default_creds.context: docker_example/docker-compose.yml ships the bundled Postgres with POSTGRES_USER=langflow / POSTGRES_PASSWORD=langflow and LANGFLOW_DATABASE_URL=postgresql://langflow:langflow@postgres:5432/langflow, with port 5432 published to the host. Not Langflow app creds (default AUTO_LOGIN needs none) but a default-credential DB exposed alongside it.
.env.example example value LANGFLOW_SUPERUSER=admin is only consulted when AUTO_LOGIN=False; at the shipped default no superuser password exists because AUTO_LOGIN=True auto-elevates every visitor.

install_tell: docker run -p 7860:7860 langflowai/langflow:latest   (or:  uv pip install langflow -U && uv run langflow run)
misconfig_patterns: AUTO_LOGIN left at default True while port 7860 is internet-exposed (vendor explicitly warns against this) -> open superuser UI
Pre-1.3.0 instances still online -> wormable unauth RCE (CVE-2025-3248)
Pre-1.7.0 with default CORS -> CVE-2025-34291 drive-by ATO
Default/unset LANGFLOW_SECRET_KEY -> forgeable session/encryption material across restarts
docker_example compose published with 5432 default-cred Postgres reachable
LANGFLOW_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE unset uses default 5 but only guards /login; AUTO_LOGIN deploys never see a login form so brute-force protection is moot
SSRF protection ships disabled (LANGFLOW_SSRF_PROTECTION_ENABLED default false)

active_probe.false_positive_check: Do NOT fingerprint on generic strings ('flow', 'AI', 'chat', 'workflow') which collide with thousands of unrelated SPAs. Confirm with the unauthenticated GET /api/v1/version JSON body containing the literal {\"package\":\"Langflow\"} field plus a semver \"main_version\" — this field name pair is vendor-unique and is served without auth on every version. Secondary confirm: GET /health_check returns {\"status\":\"ok\",\"chat\":\"ok\",\"db\":\"ok\"} (Langflow-specific triad). The favicon hash -1206067954 appears in the Shodan facet but is shared by the React/Vite default in some builds, so it must be corroborated by /api/v1/version, never used alone. A 200 HTML title 'Langflow' with a redirect to /login (no auto-login) means a hardened AUTO_LOGIN=False deploy, not the default posture — do not classify those as open.

deployment_tells: Port 7860 open with React SPA title 'Langflow'
Bundled Postgres on 5432 with default langflow:langflow creds when docker_example compose is used
No /login redirect on root = AUTO_LOGIN=True default (open superuser UI)
LANGFLOW_CONFIG_DIR=/app/langflow volume holds langflow.db (SQLite) + secret key when not on Postgres
MCP server endpoints exposed (LANGFLOW_MCP_COMPOSER_ENABLED default true) — MCP path remains unauth even in 1.5+
pivot_paths: GET /api/v1/version -> exact build for CVE scoping (1.3.0 / 1.5 / 1.6.9 / 1.7.0 cut lines)
GET /api/v1/flows/ -> enumerate flow names/IDs (business-logic + which providers are wired) without touching the LLM keys
GET /api/v1/variables/ -> list Global Variable NAMES (key inventory) — names are the finding; do not read values
GET /api/v1/auto_login -> confirm whether default auto-superuser is live (200 with token = open UI)
GET /health_check -> confirm db:ok and that a backing store is reachable
If Postgres 5432 also exposed: langflow:langflow is the documented default — note as a chained read-only DB exposure, do not connect
vulnerabilities: CVE-2025-3248 (CVSS 9.8, CISA KEV) — Unauthenticated RCE via /api/v1/validate/code: exec() of attacker Python via decorator/expression bypass; no auth required. Affects < 1.3.0. Mass-exploited to deploy the Flodrix botnet (Trend Micro, Jun 2025).

CVE-2025-34291 (CVSS 9.4, CISA KEV) — Account takeover + RCE chain: CORS allow_origins='*' with allow_credentials=True plus refresh-token cookie SameSite=None and no CSRF on token refresh; one victim page-visit yields authenticated code-exec. Affects <= 1.6.9, fixed 1.7.0 (GHSA-577h-p2hh-v4mv).
GHSA-vwmf-pq79-vjvx — Unauthenticated RCE via public flow build endpoint (2026).
GHSA-v5ff-9q35-q26f — Unauthenticated RCE in shareable playgrounds (2026).
GHSA-g2j9-7rj2-gm6c — Arbitrary file write -> RCE via v2 API (2026).
GHSA-9whx-c884-c68q — Path traversal in Knowledge Bases API bulk-delete (2026).
GHSA-rcjh-r59h-gq37 — Unauthenticated file read in playgrounds (2026).
GHSA-7grx-3xcx-2xv5 — Unauthenticated IDOR on image downloads (2026).
GHSA-9c59-2mvc-vfr8 — IDOR/BOLA across 7 Monitor API endpoints (2026).
auth_evidence: Documented quickstart sets ZERO auth env vars: README.md says `docker run -p 7860:7860 langflowai/langflow:latest` and `uv run langflow run`, both with no LANGFLOW_SUPERUSER/PASSWORD/AUTO_LOGIN. The default code value is LANGFLOW_AUTO_LOGIN=True (confirmed docs.langflow.org/api-keys-and-authentication: 'If LANGFLOW_AUTO_LOGIN=True (default) ... the visual editor automatically signs in all users as superusers'). Result: no login screen, every browser visitor lands in the flow builder as superuser. The shipped `.env.example` leaves LANGFLOW_AUTO_LOGIN=, LANGFLOW_SUPERUSER=, and LANGFLOW_SUPERUSER_PASSWORD= all EMPTY (no admin password is provisioned by default). VERSION SPLIT for the API layer: in Langflow <1.5 the REST API was also fully unauthenticated at default (the population CVE-2025-3248 reaped); v1.5 introduced API-key enforcement; v1.6 default is AUTO_LOGIN=True + SKIP_AUTH_AUTO_LOGIN=False, so UI stays open-superuser while most /api/v1 endpoints require an x-api-key. Vendor's own warning: 'Never expose Langflow ports directly to the internet without proper security measures. Set LANGFLOW_AUTO_LOGIN=False.' Primary sources: README.md quickstart, .env.example, docs.langflow.org/api-keys-and-authentication.
sensitive_assets: Upstream LLM provider API keys (OpenAI/Anthropic/Azure/etc.) stored as Langflow Global Variables and embedded inside flow JSON — readable via /api/v1/variables/ and /api/v1/flows/ when UI/API is open
Vector-store and database connection strings + credentials wired into RAG components (Pinecone/Astra/pgvector/Chroma) inside flow definitions
Ingested RAG source documents and any PII/business data uploaded via the File component (/api/v1/files/)
Full flow/agent definitions revealing proprietary prompt engineering, tool wiring, and business logic (/api/v1/flows/)
The bundled Postgres (port 5432, langflow:langflow) holding all of the above when docker_example compose is used
On <1.5 default or AUTO_LOGIN-open hosts: superuser session = arbitrary Python execution = host RCE and cloud-credential pivot
sources: [CANDIDATE - NuClide Cat-34 rag-app-builders Stage -1 OSINT 2026-06-12; software-default characterization, no live host probed yet]
